things.name: Miniatur Wunderland
things.description: The world's largest model railway exhibition with over 15,000 metres of track and 1,040 trains

things.name: Elbphilharmonie
things.description: Stunningly beautiful concert hall on the Elbe with a unique wave-shaped roof and public viewing plaza

things.name: Speicherstadt Warehouse District
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage red-brick canal warehouse district from the 1880s — now galleries and museums

things.name: Reeperbahn & St Pauli
things.description: Hamburg's famous red-light entertainment district where the Beatles began — clubs, music and theatre

things.name: Hamburg Harbour & Landungsbrucken
things.description: Germany's largest port with harbour cruises, the historic Cap San Diego ship and Sunday fish market

things.name: Planten un Blomen
things.description: Beautiful 47-hectare inner-city park with Japanese garden, tropical greenhouse and summer water-light shows

things.name: Alster Lakes
things.description: Inner and Outer Alster lakes at the city's heart — boat hire, waterfront cafes and the Jungfernstieg promenade

things.name: Hamburger Kunsthalle
things.description: One of Germany's largest art museums covering Old Masters through to contemporary art across three buildings

things.name: Blankenese Elbhang Villas
things.description: Charming historic village suburb on the Elbe with steep lanes, boat trips and riverside dining

things.name: Hamburg Rathaus (City Hall)
things.description: Magnificent 1897 neo-Renaissance city hall with 647 rooms and a striking copper-roof tower
